experiment_types: Affinity purification coupled with mass spectrometry proteomics

publications.ref: Tammsalu T, Matic I, Jaffray EG, Ibrahim AF, Tatham MH, Hay RT; Proteome-wide identification of SUMO2 modification sites., Sci Signal, 2014 Apr 29, 7, 323, rs2,

Characteristics[CellLine].note: Anchor database indicates Hek-293 cell line, but not explicitly mentioned in manuscript text

Characteristics[Label].evidence: expression of polyhistidine (6His)-tagged SUMO2 with Thr90 mutated to Lys

Characteristics[GeneticModification].value: SUMO2 with Thr90 mutated to Lys
Characteristics[GeneticModification].evidence: expression of polyhistidine (6His)-tagged SUMO2 with Thr90 mutated to Lys

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: Mass spectrometry analysis of SUMO enriched peptides

Characteristics[CleavageAgent].evidence: digested for 16 hours with Lys-C (Wako) in 50 μl IP buffer at 37 °C (enzyme to protein ratio 1:50)... subsequently digested with endoproteinase Glu-C for 16 hours in 50 μl of IP buffer at 20°C (enzyme to protein ratio 1:100)... trypsinized

Characteristics[AlkylationReagent].evidence: treated with 50 mM chloroacetamide in UA buffer for 20 minutes in the dark... treated with 50 mM chloroacetamide for 1.5 h at 20 °C

Characteristics[ReductionReagent].evidence: cell lysis buffer (6 M guanidinium-HCl, 100 mM sodium phosphate buffer pH 8.0, 10 mM Tris-HCl, 20 mM imidazole, 5 mM β-mercaptoethanol)... wash buffer pH 8.0 (8 M urea, 100 mM sodium phosphate buffer pH 8.0, 10 mM Tris-HCl, 10 mM imidazole, 5 mM β-mercaptoethanol)

Characteristics[Label].evidence: No labeling reagents mentioned in the experimental procedures

Characteristics[Modification].evidence: Carbamidomethylation of Cys was set as a fixed modification

Characteristics[Modification].1.evidence: oxidation of Met... were set as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].2.evidence: acetylation of protein N-termini... were set as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].3.evidence: phosphorylation of Ser, Thr, and Tyr... were set as variable modifications

Characteristics[Modification].4.evidence: diGly adduction to Lys (except in peptide C-terminus) were set as variable modifications

Comment[EnrichmentMethod].value: nickel affinity chromatography, anti-K-ε-GG immunoaffinity purification
Comment[EnrichmentMethod].evidence: Nickel affinity purification of 6His-SUMO2T90K conjugates was performed with Ni2+-NTA agarose beads... Enrichment of diGly-Lys containing peptides with anti-KεGG was performed... Anti-K-ε-GG conjugated to protein A beads

Comment[FractionationMethod].evidence: TAP-SUMO2 protein samples... were fractionated by protein electrophoresis and the gel was cut into nine slices

applies_to.file_pattern: TAP-SUMO2 samples only, not clearly specified for all files

Comment[NumberOfFractions].evidence: the gel was cut into nine slices

Comment[Separation].evidence: analysed by liquid chromatography tandem MS on a Q Exactive mass spectrometer... coupled to an EASY-nLC 1000 liquid chromatography system... Purified peptides were loaded onto 75 μm × 500 mm EASY-Spray column... with a linear gradient of 5 % to 22 % of solvent B

Comment[Instrument].evidence: analysed by liquid chromatography tandem MS on a Q Exactive mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific)

Comment[FragmentationMethod].evidence: Not explicitly mentioned in manuscript text
Comment[FragmentationMethod].note: Inferred from Q Exactive instrument which uses HCD by default

Comment[AcquisitionMethod].evidence: Up to one data dependent MS2 spectrum was acquired

Comment[IonizationType].evidence: coupled to an EASY-nLC 1000 liquid chromatography system (Thermo Scientific) via an EASY-Spray ion source

Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].evidence: Up to one data dependent MS2 spectrum was acquired with a resolution of 35,000 at m/z 400
Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer].note: Q Exactive uses orbitrap analyzer for MS2

Comment[CollisionEnergy].evidence: No collision energy values mentioned in manuscript

Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance].evidence: a mass deviation of 6 parts per million was set for main search

Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages].evidence: A maximum number of three or five missed cleavages were allowed for samples cleaved with Lys-C, or Lys-C and Glu-C, respectively
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages].note: 3 for Lys-C only samples, 5 for Lys-C and Glu-C samples

Comment[GradientTime].evidence: various gradient lengths from 90 minutes to 150 minutes were used

applies_to.T90: 90 minutes based on T90 in filenames
applies_to.T150: 150 minutes based on T150 in filenames

Comment[GradientTime].note: T90 and T150 tokens in filenames likely indicate gradient times of 90 and 150 minutes respectively

Comment[FlowRateChromatogram].evidence: Flow rate was set to 250 nl/min

Comment[FractionIdentifier].evidence: No explicit fraction information found in the provided text
Comment[FractionIdentifier].note: Raw filenames do not contain clear fraction identifiers, though they contain various experimental conditions (T90, T150, different enzymes)

Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].evidence: No explicit biological replicate information found in filenames or text
Characteristics[BiologicalReplicate].note: Cannot definitively identify biological replicates from filename patterns alone

Characteristics[NumberOfBiologicalReplic.evidence: Cannot determine number of biological replicates from available information

Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.evidence: Files 20130912_04_qEx_TT_T150_IP_lysC_IT1s_T1.raw and 20130912_09_qEx_TT_T150_IP_gluC_IT1s_T1.raw both contain '_T1' suffix suggesting technical replicates

applies_to.file_pattern: Files with _T1 suffix may represent technical replicates

Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica.note: Limited evidence from filename patterns, '_T1' and 'T5' suffixes in some files may indicate technical replicates

Characteristics[NumberOfSamples].evidence: Total count of raw data files listed: 10 unique .raw files
